title: Cat Head Tilt: Causes, Urgency, and Triage Advice
meta_desc: Noticing your cat tilting their head? Learn about common causes like ear infections or vestibular disease and when to seek veterinary care.
content_html:
What Is a Head Tilt in Cats?

A head tilt occurs when a cat's head is consistently leaned or rotated to one side, rather than being held upright. This is often a sign of an issue with the vestibular system, which is the biological mechanism responsible for maintaining balance. This system involves parts of the inner ear and the brain. When it is disrupted, your cat may feel dizzy, stumble, or hold their head at an unusual angle.

Is It an Emergency?

The urgency level for a head tilt is generally considered Medium. While it is rarely an immediate life-threatening emergency like a respiratory arrest, it is a sign of a significant underlying medical or neurological issue that requires professional attention. You should contact your veterinarian for an appointment within 24 hours. However, if the head tilt is accompanied by an inability to stand, severe vomiting, or a loss of consciousness, you should visit an emergency clinic immediately.

Why a Photo or Video Helps Triage

When you contact a triage professional or your local vet, providing a clear photo or a short video of your cat in their home environment is incredibly helpful. Cats often mask their symptoms or behave differently due to stress when they arrive at a clinic. Capturing a video of the head tilt, along with any eye flicking or balance issues, allows the veterinary team to assess the severity of the neurological signs and prioritize your cat's care more effectively.

faqs.question: Can an ear infection cause a head tilt?
faqs.answer: Yes, deep ear infections are one of the most common causes of head tilts in cats because they cause inflammation near the inner ear's balance sensors.

faqs.question: Is head tilt in cats painful?
faqs.answer: The tilt itself is usually a sign of disorientation or dizziness rather than pain, but the underlying cause—such as an infection, tumor, or polyp—can be very uncomfortable or painful for your pet.

faqs.question: Will my cat's head tilt go away on its own?
faqs.answer: While some conditions like idiopathic vestibular syndrome may improve over time, most causes require specific medical treatment. Leaving a head tilt untreated can lead to permanent damage or worsening of the underlying condition.
